commands.guard: HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 python3 -m compileall -q hipengine tests scripts && HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 pytest -q tests/test_generation_batch_scheduler.py tests/test_generation_qwen35_paro.py tests/test_qwen35_resident_batch_layout.py tests/test_kvcache_policy.py tests/test_kvcache_spans.py tests/test_server_api.py -q && HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 python3 scripts/qwen35_batch_correctness.py --rows 2 --json /tmp/hipengine-e2e-primitive-c2.json && HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 python3 scripts/qwen35_batch_correctness.py --rows 8 --json /tmp/hipengine-e2e-primitive-c8.json

conclusion: C8 drop-layer15 is not safe: explicit rowchunk layers 3,7,11,19,23,35,39 failed generated-token equality at [137,137,137,137,137,31,137,137] with row5 first mismatch 31. The current c8 drop27/drop31 default 3,7,11,15,19,23,35,39 stayed green at [137]*8, and the current no-flag c2/c4/c8 matrix stayed green (c2 [137,137], c4 [137]*4, c8 [137]*8). This establishes layer 15 as required for the current c8 rowchunk boundary; no code/default change and no retained throughput/scaling claim.

prompt_verifier.evidence: PASS: this materially advances focused c8 full-attention runtime evidence by proving the drop-layer15 attempt is red while the current c8 default and c2/c4/c8 generated-token equality remain green on GPU1/XTX. It is runtime artifact evidence, not schema/docs-only work, and no retained performance claim is made.
